title: Classroom challenges
noResults: Run a simulation first to verify your answers
resetConfirm: Reset all challenge progress? All completed records will be cleared.

low_abandon.hint1: High abandon rate means customers can't get seats. Try adding more seats.
low_abandon.hint2: Shortening dining time also speeds up turnover.
low_abandon.hint3: Slowing down the arrival rate (larger interval) also helps.
low_abandon.explanation: When seats are plentiful or arrivals are slow, almost no one waits long enough to leave. The key is keeping seat utilization in a reasonable range.

high_served.hint1: You need enough simulation time and fast enough service.
high_served.hint2: Adding seats and staff both boost throughput.
high_served.hint3: Shortening the arrival interval brings more people in.
high_served.explanation: Throughput = min(arrival rate, service capacity). Getting 50+ requires both ends to keep up.

staff_efficiency.hint1: Too many staff means low utilization; too few means high abandonment.
staff_efficiency.hint2: Try keeping staff count low but giving enough seats.
staff_efficiency.hint3: Find the sweet spot where staff are busy but not overwhelmed.
staff_efficiency.explanation: This is the classic efficiency vs. service quality trade-off. Kingman's approximation shows wait times explode once utilization passes ~85%.

cat_paradise.prompt: Cat visit rate > 80% with abandon rate < 20%
cat_paradise.hint1: Too few cats means not everyone gets visited. Add more cats.
cat_paradise.hint2: Shorter cat idle intervals mean more frequent visits.
cat_paradise.hint3: Cat visits extend stay time, so you might need more seats.
cat_paradise.explanation: Cat visit rate is mainly driven by cat count and idle interval. But visits lengthen customer stays, reducing seat turnover.

balanced_cafe.prompt: Balanced: abandon rate < 10%, seat/staff utilization > 50%, cat visit rate > 50%
balanced_cafe.hint1: This challenge requires all metrics to pass at once.
balanced_cafe.hint2: Start with the basics: enough seats and staff to prevent abandonment.
balanced_cafe.hint3: Then tune cat count to ensure visit rate.
balanced_cafe.explanation: A balanced cafe needs all three resource types (seats/staff/cats) properly sized. Any excess drags down its utilization.

speed_service.prompt: Avg wait for seat < 2 min AND avg wait for order < 3 min, while serving 30+
speed_service.hint1: Seat wait time depends on seat count and arrival rate.
speed_service.hint2: Order wait depends on staff count and prep time. Add staff or shorten prep.
speed_service.hint3: If throughput is too low, extend simulation time or increase arrivals.
speed_service.explanation: Fast service requires ample resources. Wait times relate directly to utilization (Kingman's formula).

max_throughput.hint1: High arrival rate + high service capacity needed. Shorten arrival interval.
max_throughput.hint2: Seats and staff both need to be generous. Under 5% abandon is strict.
max_throughput.hint3: Extending simulation time accumulates more served customers.
max_throughput.explanation: Very low abandon rate + high throughput requires the system to be far from overload. Typically needs utilization below 75%.

minimal_resources.prompt: Use minimal resources (seats + staff + cats, smallest total) to achieve: abandon rate < 15%, serve 40+
minimal_resources.hint1: Fewer resources means higher utilization, but don't overload.
minimal_resources.hint2: Cat count can be minimal (1) since there's no visit rate target.
minimal_resources.hint3: The key is finding the minimum viable seat and staff configuration.
minimal_resources.explanation: Resource minimization is a core operations research problem. Each extra unit has a cost, but too few loses customers.
